text: PPARs are nuclear receptors that among many other functions regulate genes involved in cholesterol uptake and transport (Xie, Yang, and DePierre 2002), (Gazouli 2002), (Campioli et al. 2011). The indirect link of PPAR receptors in regulation of the cholesterol transport in mitochondria derives from studies demonstrating PPARα dependent control of TSOP (Gazouli 2002), (Campioli et al. 2011). PPARα is present in steroidogenic cells e.g. of the testes during its development as well as in adult testes (Schultz et al. 1999), (Boberg et al. 2008) and modulation of its activity has been shown to impact on TSOP transcriptional activity (Gazouli 2002). The exact mechanisms of this relationship are not known.
